title: Input Enhancement in String Matching: The Horspool Algorithm
description: A visual representation of how the bad-symbol shift table is built and used to skip unnecessary comparisons, enhancing the input for efficient string matching.
relationships: The Pattern String is preprocessed to build the Bad-Symbol Shift Table. The Comparison Pointer aligns the pattern with the text. On a mismatch, the shift value from the table (based on the text character under the pattern's last character) determines how far to move the pointer for the next attempt, skipping many non-viable alignments.
